videoJson.name: 2" Helical
videoJson.fact1: This format targeted  consumers; the tapes were much smaller and  cheaper than the 2" Quad  used in the broadcast  industry
videoJson.fact2: The VR-660  (the professional version of the 2" helical scan deck) weighed 100 pounds, which was considered light in comparision with other video recorders from the same era
videoJson.fact3:
The 2" tape runs at 3.7
inches per second
videoJson.fact4: "Changing the angle that the 4 heads "the quad" recorded and read information on the tape from a 90° angle to a much longer angle (think diagonal, like the hypoteneuse of a triangle) allowed for more information to be written on the tape in the same amount of space (needing less tape for the same length of content)."
